original_promise: Labour will get Britain building again, creating jobs across England, with 1.5 million new homes over the next parliament.
simplified_promise: Build 1.5 million new homes within the 2024_2029 parliament.
keywords: 1.5 million homes, housebuilding, new homes, housing target, housing completions
measurable_outcome: Annual net housing completions in England reaching cumulative total of 1.5 million by 2029
possible_evidence_sources: MHCLG Housing Supply Statistics; ONS Housing Statistics; UK Parliament Bills API (Planning and Infrastructure Bill)

original_promise: We will immediately update the National Policy Planning Framework to undo damaging Conservative changes, including restoring mandatory housing targets.
simplified_promise: Restore mandatory local housing targets by updating the NPPF immediately after taking office.
keywords: National Planning Policy Framework, NPPF, mandatory housing targets, local plans, planning reform
measurable_outcome: Publication of revised NPPF; reinstatement of mandatory housing targets in local authority plans
possible_evidence_sources: MHCLG policy publications; legislation.gov.uk; UK Parliament Written Statements; gov.uk planning policy guidance

original_promise: Labour will take tough action to ensure that planning authorities have up-to-date Local Plans and reform and strengthen the presumption in favour of sustainable development.
simplified_promise: Require all planning authorities to maintain up-to-date Local Plans; strengthen the presumption in favour of development.
keywords: Local Plans, planning authorities, presumption in favour of sustainable development, planning reform, local planning
measurable_outcome: Number of local authorities with adopted, up-to-date Local Plans; use of presumption in favour of development
possible_evidence_sources: MHCLG Local Plans monitoring data; Planning Portal; UK Parliament Bills API; gov.uk planning statistics

original_promise: Labour will support local authorities by funding additional planning officers, through increasing the rate of the stamp duty surcharge paid by non-UK residents.
simplified_promise: Fund additional planning officers in local authorities, financed through a higher stamp duty surcharge on non-UK resident buyers.
keywords: planning officers, local authority capacity, stamp duty surcharge, non-UK residents, planning resources
measurable_outcome: Number of new planning officer posts funded; change in stamp duty surcharge rate for non-UK residents
possible_evidence_sources: HMRC Stamp Duty Land Tax statistics; MHCLG budget documents; HM Treasury Autumn Statement/Budget documents; Local Government Association data

original_promise: Labour will take a brownfield first approach, prioritising the development of previously used land wherever possible, and fast-tracking approval of urban brownfield sites.
simplified_promise: Introduce a brownfield-first planning approach and fast-track approvals for urban brownfield sites.
keywords: brownfield, previously developed land, brownfield register, fast-track approval, urban regeneration
measurable_outcome: Proportion of new homes built on brownfield land annually; speed of planning decisions on brownfield sites
possible_evidence_sources: MHCLG Brownfield Land Register data; ONS Land Use Statistics; UK Parliament Bills API (Planning and Infrastructure Bill); MHCLG planning decision statistics

original_promise: Without changing its purpose or general extent, Labour will take a more strategic approach to greenbelt land designation and release to build more homes in the right places. The release of lower quality 'grey belt' land will be prioritised and we will introduce 'golden rules' to ensure development benefits communities and nature.
simplified_promise: Introduce 'grey belt' land category within the Green Belt and apply 'golden rules' to ensure community and environmental benefit from any release.
keywords: grey belt, green belt, golden rules, greenbelt release, land designation, planning policy
measurable_outcome: Publication of grey belt definition in revised NPPF; number of grey belt sites approved; application of golden rules in planning decisions
possible_evidence_sources: MHCLG NPPF documentation; planning appeal decisions on legislation.gov.uk; UK Parliament Hansard debates; Planning Inspector decision letters

original_promise: Labour will deliver the biggest increase in social and affordable housebuilding in a generation. We will strengthen planning obligations to provide more affordable homes; make changes to the Affordable Homes Programme to ensure that it delivers more homes from existing funding; and support councils and housing associations to build their capacity and make a greater contribution to affordable housing supply.
simplified_promise: Deliver the largest increase in social and affordable housing in a generation by strengthening planning obligations and reforming the Affordable Homes Programme.
keywords: affordable housing, social housing, Affordable Homes Programme, Section 106, planning obligations, housing associations, councils
measurable_outcome: Annual number of affordable homes delivered; proportion of new homes that are affordable; Homes England programme delivery data
possible_evidence_sources: Homes England Affordable Homes Programme statistics; MHCLG affordable housing supply data; UK Parliament Bills API; Budget/Spending Review documents

original_promise: Labour will prioritise the building of new social rented homes and better protect our existing stock by reviewing the increased right to buy discounts introduced in 2012 and increasing protections on newly-built social housing.
simplified_promise: Prioritise new social rented homes and review Right to Buy discounts to protect social housing stock.
keywords: social rented homes, Right to Buy, council housing, social housing stock, housing associations, right to buy discounts
measurable_outcome: Number of new social rented homes built annually; changes to Right to Buy discount levels; net social housing stock change
possible_evidence_sources: MHCLG Social Housing Supply Statistics; Shelter/National Housing Federation reports; legislation.gov.uk (Housing Act amendments); Hansard

original_promise: Labour will work with local authorities to give first-time buyers the first chance to buy homes and end the farce of entire developments being sold off to international investors before houses are even built.
simplified_promise: Give first-time buyers priority access to new housing developments and restrict bulk sales of new homes to international investors.
keywords: first-time buyers, priority purchase, international investors, bulk sales, new homes, overseas buyers
measurable_outcome: Legislation or planning policy restricting pre-sale to overseas investors; monitoring share of new homes sold to first-time buyers
possible_evidence_sources: UK Parliament Bills API; legislation.gov.uk; HMRC Stamp Duty data by buyer type; MHCLG housing policy documents

original_promise: We will introduce a permanent, comprehensive mortgage guarantee scheme, to support first-time buyers who struggle to save for a large deposit, with lower mortgage costs.
simplified_promise: Launch a permanent mortgage guarantee scheme for first-time buyers with small deposits.
keywords: mortgage guarantee, first-time buyers, deposit, Help to Buy, mortgage scheme, low deposit
measurable_outcome: Launch and terms of scheme; number of mortgages guaranteed annually; average deposit size for participating buyers
possible_evidence_sources: HM Treasury Budget/Spending Review documents; UK Finance mortgage data; gov.uk housing schemes page; ONS House Price Index

original_promise: Labour will immediately abolish Section 21 'no fault' evictions, prevent private renters being exploited and discriminated against, empower them to challenge unreasonable rent increases, and take steps to decisively raise standards, including extending 'Awaab's Law' to the private sector.
simplified_promise: Abolish Section 21 no-fault evictions and extend Awaab's Law to the private rented sector.
keywords: Section 21, no-fault evictions, private rented sector, renters rights, Awaab's Law, rent increases, discrimination, tenancy reform
measurable_outcome: Royal Assent of Renters' Rights Bill abolishing Section 21; commencement date of abolition; number of Section 21 notices served post-legislation
possible_evidence_sources: UK Parliament Bills API (Renters' Rights Bill); legislation.gov.uk; MHCLG Rents statistics; Ministry of Justice landlord possession statistics

original_promise: Labour will empower renters to challenge unreasonable rent increases.
simplified_promise: Give renters a legal right to challenge unreasonable rent increases.
keywords: rent increases, rent tribunal, First-tier Tribunal, rent challenge, private rented sector, tenancy reform
measurable_outcome: Royal Assent of relevant legislation; number of rent challenges lodged at First-tier Tribunal annually
possible_evidence_sources: UK Parliament Bills API (Renters' Rights Bill); legislation.gov.uk; HMRC Private Rental Market statistics; ONS Private Rental Price Index; First-tier Tribunal statistics

original_promise: Labour will act where the Conservatives have failed and finally bring the feudal leasehold system to an end. We will enact the package of Law Commission proposals on leasehold enfranchisement, right to manage and commonhold. We will take further steps to ban new leasehold flats and ensure commonhold is the default tenure.
simplified_promise: Enact Law Commission leasehold reforms; ban new leasehold flats and make commonhold the default tenure.
keywords: leasehold, commonhold, leasehold enfranchisement, right to manage, Law Commission, leasehold reform, feudal, ban new leasehold
measurable_outcome: Royal Assent of Leasehold and Commonhold Reform Bill; ban on new leasehold flat sales; number of commonhold conversions
possible_evidence_sources: UK Parliament Bills API; legislation.gov.uk; Land Registry leasehold data; MHCLG leasehold reform policy documents; Law Commission publications

original_promise: We will tackle unregulated and unaffordable ground rent charges. We will act to bring the injustice of 'fleecehold' private housing estates and unfair maintenance costs to an end.
simplified_promise: Cap or ban unaffordable ground rents and end unfair 'fleecehold' maintenance charges on private estates.
keywords: ground rent, fleecehold, service charges, estate management, maintenance charges, private estates, leasehold reform
measurable_outcome: Legislation capping or banning ground rents; regulations on estate management charges; number of affected leaseholders
possible_evidence_sources: UK Parliament Bills API (Leasehold and Commonhold Reform); legislation.gov.uk; Leasehold Advisory Service data; Competition and Markets Authority reports

original_promise: Labour will develop a new cross-government strategy, working with Mayors and Councils across the country, to put Britain back on track to ending homelessness.
simplified_promise: Develop a cross-government homelessness strategy with Mayors and Councils to end homelessness.
keywords: homelessness strategy, rough sleeping, hidden homelessness, cross-government, Mayors, councils, homelessness reduction
measurable_outcome: Publication of a homelessness strategy; annual rough sleeping count; homelessness statistics (households in temporary accommodation)
possible_evidence_sources: MHCLG Rough Sleeping Statistics; MHCLG Statutory Homelessness Statistics; gov.uk policy documents; ONS homelessness data; Shelter/Crisis reports

original_promise: Labour will also take decisive action to improve building safety, including through regulation, to ensure we never again see a repeat of the Grenfell fire. We will review how to better protect leaseholders from costs and take steps to accelerate the pace of remediation across the country. We will put a renewed focus on ensuring those responsible for the building safety crisis pay to put it right.
simplified_promise: Accelerate cladding remediation and ensure building safety costs fall on developers, not leaseholders.
keywords: building safety, cladding, remediation, Grenfell, leaseholders, developers, Building Safety Act, remediation fund
measurable_outcome: Number of buildings with unsafe cladding remediated; proportion of remediation costs met by developers vs leaseholders; pace of remediation progress
possible_evidence_sources: MHCLG Building Safety Remediation statistics; Building Safety Regulator annual reports; UK Parliament Bills API; legislation.gov.uk; National Audit Office reports

original_promise: Labour will require all Combined and Mayoral Authorities to strategically plan for housing growth in their areas. We will give Combined Authorities new planning powers along with new freedoms and flexibilities to make better use of grant funding.
simplified_promise: Require Combined and Mayoral Authorities to produce strategic housing growth plans and give them new planning powers.
keywords: Combined Authorities, Mayoral Authorities, strategic planning, housing growth, devolution, planning powers, cross-boundary planning
measurable_outcome: Number of Combined/Mayoral Authorities with adopted strategic housing plans; new planning powers conferred by legislation
possible_evidence_sources: UK Parliament Bills API (English Devolution Bill/Planning and Infrastructure Bill); MHCLG devolution policy documents; Hansard; Combined Authority annual reports

original_promise: Labour will further reform compulsory purchase compensation rules to improve land assembly, speed up site delivery, and deliver housing, infrastructure, amenity, and transport benefits in the public interest. We will take steps to ensure that for specific types of development schemes, landowners are awarded fair compensation rather than inflated prices based on the prospect of planning permission.
simplified_promise: Reform compulsory purchase rules so landowners receive fair value, not inflated hope value, enabling faster land assembly for housing.
keywords: compulsory purchase order, CPO, land assembly, hope value, fair compensation, planning reform, land value
measurable_outcome: Legislative changes to compulsory purchase compensation rules; number of CPO cases resolved; average land cost per unit on public schemes
possible_evidence_sources: UK Parliament Bills API (Planning and Infrastructure Bill); legislation.gov.uk; MHCLG planning reform documents; Land Registry land price data; Homes England land assembly data
